trails.name: Tre Cime di Lavaredo Loop
trails.tagline: Venture into the heart of the mountains
trails.description: One of the most iconic hikes in the Dolomites, the Tre Cime Loop offers breathtaking 360-degree views of the famous three peaks. Starting from Rifugio Auronzo, this well-maintained trail circles the massive limestone towers, passing crystal-clear alpine lakes and meadows filled with wildflowers in summer. The trail is relatively gentle, making it accessible to most hikers while still delivering world-class alpine scenery. Don't miss the stunning reflections in Lago dei Piani and the dramatic north faces of the peaks.

pois.name: Rifugio Auronzo
pois.description: Starting point with parking and refreshments

pois.name: Lago dei Piani
pois.description: Beautiful alpine lake with peak reflections

trails.name: Lago di Braies Circular Trail
trails.description: Experience the magic of one of the most photographed lakes in the Alps. This gentle walk around Lago di Braies (Pragser Wildsee) showcases the lake's stunning turquoise waters framed by the dramatic Seekofel peak. The trail meanders through fragrant pine forests and opens to shoreline sections where you can admire the traditional wooden boats floating on the crystal-clear water. Perfect for families and those seeking an easy introduction to alpine hiking, this trail offers maximum beauty with minimal effort. Early morning visits reward hikers with mirror-like reflections and fewer crowds.

pois.name: Boat Rental Dock
pois.description: Famous photo spot with wooden boats

trails.name: Sentiero del Viel del Pan
trails.tagline: Venture into the heart of the mountains
trails.description: A historic WWI trail following the Path of Bread, where supplies were transported to soldiers during the Great War. This spectacular route traverses beneath the Sella massif and Marmolada glacier, offering constant views of the Dolomites' most impressive peaks. The trail features well-maintained paths carved into the rock face, with several tunnels and galleries adding adventure to the journey. Along the way, discover war relics, memorials, and interpretive signs telling the story of the mountain war. The ever-changing perspectives of the Marmolada glacier make this a photographer's paradise.

pois.name: Passo Pordoi
pois.description: Starting point with cable car access

pois.name: WWI Trenches
pois.description: Preserved World War I military positions

trails.name: Alpe di Siusi Meadow Walk
trails.description: Europe's largest high-altitude alpine meadow provides the stunning backdrop for this peaceful ramble. Rolling across flower-dotted grasslands with the jagged peaks of Sassolungo and Sassopiatto dominating the skyline, this trail epitomizes the romance of the Alps. Traditional Tyrolean farmhouses dot the landscape, and the gentle terrain makes it ideal for families and those seeking a relaxing mountain experience. In spring and summer, the meadows burst with wildflowers, while autumn brings golden larches. The crisp mountain air and sweeping vistas create an unforgettable sensory experience.

pois.name: Compatsch Cable Car Station
pois.description: Main access point to Alpe di Siusi

pois.name: Sassolungo Viewpoint
pois.description: Classic view of the iconic peak

trails.name: Seceda Ridge Trail
trails.tagline: Venture into the heart of the mountains
trails.description: Seceda is one of the most photographed peaks in the Dolomites, and this ridge trail showcases why. Starting from the Seceda cable car station, the trail follows the dramatic jagged ridge with breathtaking views of the Odle/Geisler peaks on one side and the Puez massif on the other. The unique geological formations create a surreal landscape of sharp limestone spires and rolling alpine meadows. Summer brings carpets of wildflowers, while autumn offers golden larches against the limestone. This trail rewards hikers with 360-degree mountain panoramas and is a photographer's dream, especially during sunrise and sunset.

pois.name: Seceda Cable Car Station
pois.description: Starting point with iconic ridge views

pois.name: Odle Viewpoint
pois.description: Best view of the Geisler peaks

trails.name: Lago di Carezza Trail
trails.description: Discover the Rainbow Lake, as Lago di Carezza is locally known, famous for its stunning color palette that changes throughout the day. Nestled in a pine forest with the towering Latemar massif reflected in its emerald-green waters, this small but spectacular alpine lake offers an easy circular trail perfect for all ages. Legend says a wizard created a rainbow to capture a water nymph's heart, and when she rejected him, he shattered it into the lake, giving it its magical colors. The trail meanders through fragrant forests with numerous viewpoints to admire the lake's ever-changing hues, from turquoise to emerald to deep blue depending on light and season.

trails.name: Puez-Odle Alta Via Trail
trails.description: A challenging high-altitude traverse through the heart of the Puez-Odle Nature Park, this trail demands fitness and rewards with unparalleled Dolomite vistas. The route climbs steeply through alpine meadows before traversing rocky terrain with cable-assisted sections beneath towering limestone walls. Hikers pass through different geological formations, showcasing millions of years of Earth's history in the stratified rock faces. Multiple mountain huts provide rest stops and refreshments, while marmots and chamois are often spotted along the route. This is true alpine hiking at its finest, combining physical challenge with spiritual reward as you traverse one of the most dramatic mountain landscapes in the Alps.

trails.name: Val di Funes Meadow Trail
trails.description: Experience one of the most iconic views in all of the Alps on this gentle valley walk. The trail offers the classic postcard perspective of the St. Johann church with the dramatic Odle/Geisler peaks rising behind it. This enchanting valley showcases the perfect harmony between human settlement and nature, with traditional Tyrolean farms scattered across rolling meadows against a backdrop of soaring limestone spires. The trail is especially magical in spring with blooming wildflowers, in autumn with golden foliage, and in early morning when mist clings to the valley floor. This easy walk captures the essence of alpine beauty and is suitable for all ages and fitness levels.

pois.name: St. Johann Church
pois.description: Famous alpine church with Odle backdrop

trails.name: Hochmuth Trail (Muthöfe Route)
trails.tagline: Venture into the heart of the mountains
trails.description: A spectacular mountain ascent from Dorf Tirol to the historic Muthöfe farms, following the ancient Trail 23 (Muter Weg). This classic South Tyrolean hike climbs through mixed forests and alpine meadows, passing traditional mountain farms that have stood for over 800 years on the steep southern slopes of Mutspitze. The trail offers constantly improving panoramic views over Merano, the Adige Valley, and the surrounding peaks. At the summit station, enjoy refreshments at Gasthaus Hochmuth while taking in breathtaking 360-degree vistas. The route combines cultural heritage with natural beauty, showcasing the traditional alpine farming lifestyle against a backdrop of dramatic mountain scenery. Starting right from Dorf Tirol, this trail is perfect for GPS tracking testing with its real-world coordinates, measurable elevation gain, and multiple checkpoint POIs along the route.

pois.name: Hochmuth Cable Car Valley Station
pois.description: Starting point with parking and cable car access

pois.name: Gasthaus Steinegg
pois.description: Traditional mountain inn at 1,406m with local cuisine

pois.name: Hochmuth Mountain Station
pois.description: Summit viewpoint with panoramic views over Merano and Adige Valley
